sources.notes: Polynomial construction/search problem with very short representation.
Official editorial splits polynomial terms into two halves and meets in the middle.

annotation.summary: Find a very short polynomial representation satisfying the target constraints.
annotation.constraints: The state space is exponential in degree/terms, so it must be split into two halves.
annotation.core_idea: Enumerate all possible contributions of the first half of terms and store their resulting vector/value. Enumerate the second half and look for the complementary value required by the target polynomial condition. Algebraic normalization removes equivalent representations and keeps the hash/table size feasible.
tricks: meet in the middle on polynomial terms

solution_variants.name: polynomial-mitm
solution_variants.summary: Split term choices into two halves and match complementary polynomial contributions by hashing.

tags.evidence: The official tags and solution split the exponential search into two halves.
Polynomial contributions are represented and matched as coefficient/evaluation vectors.
Half contributions are stored in a lookup table by normalized vector value.
